image.alt: Patients smiling at Island Orthodontics office

paragraphs.text: Welcome to Island Orthodontics, a respected orthodontic provider serving Deer Park, Ronkonkoma and Roslyn, NY. Our top priority is to deliver the highest-quality orthodontic care in a friendly, comfortable environment using the latest technology — from Invisalign clear aligners and self‑ligating systems (Empower®) to computer‑guided planning — combined with courteous, individualized service to help adults, teens and children achieve healthy, beautiful smiles. Contact any of our offices to schedule a consultation.

items.title: Advanced Technology

image.alt: Digital orthodontic planning and clear aligners

items.text: We use Invisalign, iTero 3D scanning and modern bracket systems (ceramic and self‑ligating) for precise, efficient treatment.

items.title: Personalized Care

image.alt: Orthodontic care tailored for each patient

items.text: Doctors and staff create custom treatment plans for children, teens, and adults, focused on comfort and long‑term results.

items.title: Convenient Locations

image.alt: Multiple Island Orthodontics locations serving Long Island

items.text: Offices in Deer Park, Lake Ronkonkoma and Roslyn make scheduling and follow‑up easier for local families.

items.title: Recognized Quality
items.text: Local awards and community recognition reflect our commitment to excellent patient care and outcomes.

paragraphs.text: Dr. Efraim Zak earned his DDS and completed orthodontic specialty training at New York University. He maintains an active research and continuing‑education practice and lectures on Invisalign<sup>®</sup> techniques. “I love orthodontics; it is my passion.”

Dr. Renée Nykolak earned her DMD at the University of Pennsylvania and completed specialty training with a Master of Science. She focuses on creating a fun, high‑quality treatment environment while using the newest orthodontic technologies.

Dr. Michael Kirshblum earned his DMD at the University of Pennsylvania and completed an MS in Orthodontics at Temple University. He is trained in current orthodontic technologies and dedicated to patient‑centered care.

Dr. Adam D. Shatz holds a DDS and completed an Orthodontic Residency at SUNY Stony Brook. With 17+ years of specialty experience, he remains active in professional organizations and continuing education.

Dr. Upasna Malhotra completed her DDS at Stony Brook and a three‑year orthodontic specialty program at Montefiore. She is passionate about creating enjoyable patient journeys and uses the latest technology to deliver excellent care.

Dr. Devin Zolnowski completed his DMD at Rutgers and specialty training at Maimonides Medical Center, serving as Chief Resident. He brings clinical excellence and a patient‑focused approach to treatment.

Dr. Kruno Tovilo trained at Boston University and completed post‑graduate orthodontic studies at NYU. An international lecturer, he specializes in self‑ligation biomechanics and complex interdisciplinary cases.

Dr. Bronwyn McAndrews trained at USC and completed orthodontic residency at the University of Connecticut. With 30+ years of experience, she offers conservative, comfortable treatment plans tailored to each patient.
Dr. Mike Cotzas completed his DDS at Columbia and post‑doctoral orthodontic training at Montefiore. He stays active in professional learning and applies up‑to‑date techniques in patient care.

Dr. Matthew Miller completed orthodontic specialty training at NYU and holds publications on prevention of white spots in orthodontics. He uses modern techniques to deliver predictable treatment and great results.
Dr. Princy Kuriakose earned her DDS and completed a three‑year orthodontic advanced training at the University of Washington. She is board‑certified and active in continuing education and research.
We offer modern orthodontic options to suit a variety of needs, including clear aligners, aesthetic braces, retainers, and adjunct technologies to enhance treatment efficiency.

og_meta.description: Island Orthodontics provides modern orthodontic care in Deer Park, Lake Ronkonkoma and Roslyn, NY, offering Invisalign®, braces, retainers, and convenient same‑

image.alt: Team of orthodontic professionals smiling in the Lakeshore Orthodontics office lobby

paragraphs.text: We are a respected orthodontic provider serving the Deer Park, Ronkonkoma and Roslyn, NY communities. Our practice’s top priority is to provide the highest quality orthodontic care in a friendly, comfortable environment.

We utilize the latest technological advances in the industry, for example invisible ceramic braces, Empower® self-ligating braces, and Invisalign<sup>®</sup>, along with modern computer technology to ensure you receive the most effective care possible.
We recognize every patient has different needs and pride ourselves on courteous service for adults, adolescents, and children, helping every patient achieve a healthy, beautiful smile.
Through thoughtful treatment planning, gentle techniques, and flexible scheduling, we make orthodontics accessible, comfortable, and tailored to each patient’s lifestyle.

“I love orthodontics; it is my passion. As an orthodontist, I have the chance to meet amazing families and give them something that will change their life for the better: an amazing, healthy smile.” – Dr. Zak

Dr. Efraim Zak earned his Doctorate of Dental Surgery from New York University College of Dentistry and completed his orthodontic residency at NYU. He maintains an active role in research, continuing education, and professional societies including the American Dental Association and the American Association of Orthodontists.

Dr. Devin Zolnowski grew up in New Jersey and completed his Doctor of Dental Medicine at Rutgers School of Dental Medicine. He finished specialty training in Orthodontics and Dentofacial Orthopedics at Maimonides Medical Center, serving as Chief Resident.
Outside the office he enjoys spending time with his wife, staying active, and participating in professional organizations including the American Association of Orthodontists.

Dr. Upasna Malhotra grew up on Long Island and completed her DDS at Stony Brook School of Dental Medicine followed by specialty training in Orthodontics and Dentofacial Orthopedics at Montefiore Medical Center, where she served as Chief Orthodontic Resident.
She focuses on making orthodontic treatment a positive, personalized experience and uses modern technology to deliver efficient care.

Dr. Michael Kirshblum, a Long Island native, completed his DMD at the University of Pennsylvania and advanced orthodontic training with a Master of Science from Temple University. He brings current clinical training and a patient-first approach to care.
Outside of work he enjoys time with family and following local sports.

Dr. Adam D. Shatz is a board-trained orthodontic specialist who completed his DDS and orthodontic residency at SUNY Stony Brook. With over seventeen years in practice he emphasizes continuing education and evidence-based care.
He is active in professional organizations and focuses on delivering comfortable, individualized treatment plans.

Dr. Mike Cotzas completed his dental degree at Columbia University and orthodontic training at Montefiore Medical Center. He maintains involvement in continuing education and professional associations to keep care current.
He enjoys attending conferences and bringing new techniques and technology into practice for patient benefit.

Dr. Renée Nykolak earned her DMD from the University of Pennsylvania and a Masters in Orthodontics from the University of Detroit Mercy. She is committed to high-quality care using modern techniques and technologies.
She creates a welcoming treatment environment and enjoys staying active, traveling, and spending time with family.

Dr. Matthew Miller joined Island Orthodontics in 2016 after completing orthodontic specialty training at NYU. He has contributed to research on prevention of white spots in orthodontics and stays current with new technology in the field.
His interests include tennis, swimming, and attending live events.

Dr. Princy Kuriakose completed her dental degree and advanced orthodontic training, including a Master of Science from the University of Washington. She is board-certified and engaged in ongoing professional development.
In her free time she enjoys exploring new places, baking, and staying active.

Dr. Bronwyn McAndrews trained at USC School of Dentistry and completed her orthodontic residency at the University of Connecticut. With over 30 years of experience she focuses on conservative, comfortable treatment plans.
She and her husband have traveled extensively and are the proud parents of twins.
Dr. Kruno Tovilo trained at Boston University and completed post-graduate orthodontics at New York University. He has a long history of lecturing and working with complex multidisciplinary cases.
Dr. Tovilo enjoys outdoor activities and travel, and brings a deep academic and clinical background to patient care.

image.alt: For New Patients graphic showing friendly orthodontic care

paragraphs.text: The first step toward a beautiful, healthy smile is to schedule an appointment. To request an appointment, complete the online request form and our scheduling coordinator will contact you to confirm a date and time.

The form asks for basic contact information, preferred office, reason for visit, insurance details, and appointment day preferences. If you need to cancel, reschedule, or require immediate attention, please call the office directly.

image.alt: Appointment request at orthodontic office, patient filling out form

paragraphs.text: Please complete the health-history form for your chosen office before your visit. We offer English and Spanish online forms for each location, and printable PDF forms if you prefer to bring paperwork to your appointment.

image.alt: Patient filling out orthodontic health history forms online

og_meta.title: Get Started | Island Orthodontics
og_meta.description: Start your Island Orthodontics journey: schedule an appointment, access new-patient forms, and learn what to expect at your first visit.

image.alt: Collage showing common orthodontic treatments and before-and-after photos

paragraphs.text: Traditional metal braces remain a reliable way to straighten teeth. Modern brackets and wires are smaller and more comfortable than ever, and they steadily guide teeth into healthier alignment over the course of treatment.

image.alt: Before photo showing crowded teeth prior to orthodontic treatment

paragraphs.text: Self-ligating braces use a built-in clip instead of elastic ties, which can reduce friction and sometimes shorten adjustment times. The Empower system is a self-ligating option designed for efficiency and patient comfort.

image.alt: Patient before treatment showing bite issues

paragraphs.text: Ceramic braces are made from translucent materials that blend with your teeth, offering a less visible option. Clarity ceramic brackets provide aesthetic results while still delivering reliable tooth movement; they may need a bit more attention to oral hygiene.

image.alt: Before photo of patient with missing lateral incisors prior to orthodontic treatment

paragraphs.text: Invisalign<sup>®</sup> uses a series of clear, removable aligners created from precise 3D scans. The trays move teeth in planned stages and are nearly invisible in daily life.
Because aligners are removable, you can brush and floss normally and enjoy the foods you love while progressing toward a straighter smile.

image.alt: Invisalign clear aligners on a model showing the transparent trays

paragraphs.text: Lingual braces are attached behind the teeth so they are invisible when you smile. Each set is custom-made to fit the contours of your teeth and deliver discreet correction.

image.alt: Before photo of a patient with an underbite prior to treatment

paragraphs.text: The Forsus™ appliance helps correct certain bite discrepancies, especially overbites, by applying gentle, continuous force. It’s low-profile, does not require daily patient adjustments, and can reduce the need for headgear in many cases.

image.alt: Forsus appliance shown in a clinical image attached to braces

paragraphs.text: A palatal expander gently widens the upper jaw when needed, creating space and improving bite relationships. Expansion is gradual and the device is typically worn for several months to stabilize results.

image.alt: Palatal expander device shown in a model of the upper jaw

paragraphs.text: Retainers protect the investment you made in orthodontic treatment. We offer removable and fixed retainers and will recommend the option that best preserves your bite and smile.
Consistent wear and proper care help retainers maintain tooth positions long term. We provide clear instructions and follow-up to keep your results stable.

image.alt: Clear retainers resting on a model of teeth

image.alt: Close-up of clear aligners with a smiling patient, modern orthodontic care

paragraphs.text: Find answers to common questions about Invisalign<sup>®</sup>, braces, scheduling, insurance, and retainer care at Island Orthodontics.

accordion.header: What orthodontic treatments do you offer?

paragraphs.text: We provide traditional metal braces, tooth-colored ceramic braces, and Invisalign<sup>®</sup> clear aligners for teens and adults. We also create custom retainers and offer specialty appliances when needed.

image.alt: Invisalign clear aligners on a tray with smiling patient in background

accordion.header: At what age should my child see an orthodontist?

paragraphs.text: The American Association of Orthodontists recommends an initial evaluation by age 7. Early visits let us monitor growth and, if needed, plan treatment that can make later care simpler and more effective.

image.alt: Young patient wearing braces and smiling in a bright orthodontic office

accordion.header: How long does treatment usually take?

paragraphs.text: Treatment time varies by the complexity of the case. Many patients finish care in 12 to 24 months. At your first visit we’ll review your goals and give a personalized timeline.

image.alt: Orthodontic consultation with doctor reviewing treatment plan with patient

accordion.header: What payment and insurance options are available?

paragraphs.text: We accept most major dental insurance plans and offer flexible monthly payment options to fit your budget. Our team will help estimate coverage and discuss financing at your appointment.

image.alt: Patient forms and reception desk at orthodontic office

accordion.header: How can I schedule an appointment?

paragraphs.text: Call or text (631) 588-1199, email appointments@IslandOrtho.com, or submit a request online at /appointment-request/. We’ll find a time that fits your family and direct you to the Lake Ronkonkoma office or another convenient location.

image.alt: Appointment button graphic and contact details for scheduling
